skill.name: x-article-publisher
skill.description: Publish Markdown articles to the X Articles editor by converting local Markdown to rich text, copying HTML and images through the macOS clipboard, and guiding browser automation to save a draft.
skill.summary: Convert local Markdown into an X Article draft with rich text and image placement.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many alerts, but most high-severity weak-crypto, entropy, and reconnaissance matches are false positives from words, argparse setup, comments, or documentation examples. Real risks remain because the skill intentionally runs local Python scripts, reads user-selected files, writes temporary HTML, controls the system clipboard, and opens X in a browser. No evidence found of malicious exfiltration, destructive commands, or prompt injection.

medium_findings.title: Local Script Execution and Clipboard Control
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill instructs the agent to run Python helper scripts and those scripts copy image or HTML data to the macOS clipboard. This is expected for rich-text publishing, but it gives the workflow access to local files and clipboard contents, so users should only run it on trusted Markdown and image files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples and implementation clearly show local scripts writing image and HTML data to NSPasteboard. The behavior is intentional and useful, but it is a real local data-handling risk.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem Reads and Temporary File Workflow
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill reads local Markdown, image, and HTML files and recommends writing generated HTML to /tmp. This is necessary for the workflow, but it may expose sensitive article content or local paths if users process untrusted or private files without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The parser and clipboard helper directly read paths supplied by the user, and the instructions explicitly use a temporary HTML file. The risk is bounded to local file handling, not hidden collection.

medium_findings.title: Unescaped Markdown-to-HTML Conversion
medium_findings.description: NEEDS REVIEW: The converter builds HTML with regular expressions and inserts Markdown link text, href values, and paragraph content without HTML escaping. This can preserve unsafe markup or misleading links in the draft when the input Markdown is untrusted, even though no direct code execution was observed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The conversion function directly interpolates Markdown content into HTML strings. Browser and X editor sanitization may reduce impact, but the skill itself does not sanitize untrusted content.

low_findings.title: Weak-Crypto and Entropy Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The reported weak cryptographic algorithm matches do not identify cryptographic operations in context. The cited script lines define argparse parsers, and the SKILL.md line is metadata describing Markdown publishing; no hashing, encryption, or authentication logic was found there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain YAML metadata or argparse setup, not cryptographic API calls. The entropy alert is explained by mixed natural language and code examples in documentation.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The reported reconnaissance lines are part of title extraction comments and logic in the Markdown parser. No commands collect hostnames, users, processes, network interfaces, or other system inventory.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant code only scans Markdown lines to choose an article title. There is no system API or shell call that performs host reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded X URL Is Expected
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The hardcoded network destination is the X Articles compose page required by the skill. No evidence found that article data or credentials are sent to an unrelated endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only documented URL is the expected X compose endpoint for the publishing workflow. The scripts do not make network requests.

low_findings.title: Hidden Directory References Are Installation Paths
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE WITH RESIDUAL RISK: The hidden home-directory references point to the expected Claude skill installation path. This is not stealthy persistence, but it still means users execute scripts from a hidden tool directory.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The references consistently identify where the skill scripts are installed and how to invoke them. I did not find writes to hidden startup files or persistence mechanisms.

dangerous_patterns.title: Runs Local Python Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill depends on command-line Python helpers for parsing Markdown and copying data to the clipboard. This is functional but should be disclosed as local code execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documentation explicitly instructs running Python scripts. The commands are not obfuscated, but they are executable local actions.

dangerous_patterns.title: Direct Clipboard Mutation
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper script clears the general macOS pasteboard and writes HTML or image data to it. Users may lose current clipboard contents and should avoid untrusted inputs.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The implementation calls NSPasteboard.generalPasteboard(), clears contents, and writes new data. This is direct evidence of clipboard mutation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unescaped HTML Assembly
dangerous_patterns.description: The Markdown converter creates HTML through regex substitutions and string interpolation without escaping user-controlled text and URL values.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code constructs HTML with regular expressions and f-strings. The final impact depends on X editor sanitization, which is outside this skill.

content.value_statement: Publishing a formatted article from Markdown to X can be slow and error-prone. This skill converts local Markdown, places images, and saves the result as a draft for manual review.
actual_capabilities: Parses local Markdown files into title, cover image, content images, and HTML.
Uses the first Markdown image as the X Article cover image.
Copies generated HTML to the macOS clipboard for rich-text pasting.
Copies content images to the clipboard for insertion at recorded block positions.
Guides browser automation to open the X Articles editor and save a draft.
Preserves common Markdown formatting such as headings, lists, links, bold text, and blockquotes.
limitations: Requires macOS clipboard APIs, Python dependencies, and Playwright browser automation.
Requires the user to be logged in to X with access to X Articles.
Does not publish automatically; it saves a draft for manual review.
Does not sanitize untrusted Markdown before converting it to HTML.

use_cases.title: Publish Technical Essays
use_cases.description: Convert a local Markdown essay with headings, links, and images into an X Article draft.

use_cases.title: Prepare Product Updates
use_cases.description: Move a release note or product announcement from Markdown into the X editor with formatting intact.

use_cases.title: Draft Visual Articles
use_cases.description: Place multiple images after the correct text blocks while keeping the first image as the cover.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Draft
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for one Markdown file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Publish my Markdown file at [path] to X Articles as a draft. Use the first image as the cover and stop before publishing.

prompt_templates.title: Check Formatting First
prompt_templates.scenario: Review article structure before browser automation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Parse [path] and summarize the detected title, cover image, content images, and supported formatting before creating the X draft.

prompt_templates.title: Handle Several Images
prompt_templates.prompt: Publish [path] to X Articles. Insert content images in reverse block-index order so each image lands after the correct section.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Draft Verification
prompt_templates.scenario: Full workflow with manual review checkpoints.
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare an X Article draft from [path], verify the preview formatting, report any unsupported Markdown, and leave the article unpublished.

output_examples.input: A Markdown article with a title, cover image, two section images, links, and lists.
output_examples.output: The X Articles editor contains a saved draft with the title filled, the cover uploaded, rich text pasted, and images placed after their matching sections.
The editor contains a text-only X Article draft with headings, paragraphs, lists, links, and bold text preserved where supported.

output_examples.input: A Markdown article with code blocks.
output_examples.output: The draft represents code blocks as blockquotes because X Articles does not support native preformatted code blocks.

best_practices: Review the Markdown and image paths before running the clipboard helper scripts.
Preview the X Article draft and verify every image position before publishing manually.
Use trusted local files because the converter does not sanitize Markdown HTML or links.
anti_patterns: Do not run the workflow on Markdown from an unknown source without reviewing it first.
Do not publish directly from automation; keep the manual review step.
Do not rely on text matching for image placement when block indexes are available.

faq.question: Does this skill publish automatically?
faq.answer: No. It creates or updates an X Article draft and tells the user to review and publish manually.

faq.question: Which platform does the clipboard helper support?
faq.answer: The helper is written for macOS and uses AppKit pasteboard APIs through pyobjc.

faq.question: What happens to the first image in the Markdown file?
faq.answer: The first image is treated as the cover image. Later images are inserted into the article body.

faq.question: Can it preserve all Markdown formatting?
faq.answer: No. It supports common formatting, but code blocks are converted to blockquotes because X Articles has limited support.

faq.question: Does it need an X Premium subscription?
faq.answer: Yes. The user must be logged in to an X account that has access to X Articles.

faq.question: Is it safe to use with untrusted Markdown?
faq.answer: No. Review untrusted Markdown first because the converter builds HTML without sanitizing all text and links.
